Let me start by reading the file to understand the full context.

Now let me reason through the fix:

1. **The migration rule** says to replace `<Text>` with `<Content>`, moving `Text`'s props to `Content`.
2. **Line 6**: `Text` is imported from `@patternfly/react-core`.
3. **Line 62-65**: `<Text component="p" className="pf-v6-u-mb-sm">` is used in JSX.

The migration says `Text` is deprecated and should be replaced with `Content`. Looking at the JSX usage:

```jsx
<Text component="p" className="pf-v6-u-mb-sm">
  <BlueInfoCircleIcon className="pf-v6-u-mr-sm" />
  {t(`You can add ${remainingTags} more tags`)}
</Text>
```

The property mapping shows all `Text` props map directly to the same-named props on `Content`. So:
- Replace `Text` with `Content` in the import
- Replace `<Text ...>` and `</Text>` with `<Content ...>` and `</Content>` in JSX

The props (`component="p"`, `className="pf-v6-u-mb-sm"`, and children) all map directly.

arguments.after:
<Content component="p" className="pf-v6-u-mb-sm">
          <BlueInfoCircleIcon className="pf-v6-u-mr-sm" />
          {t(`You can add ${remainingTags} more tags`)}
        </Content>
arguments.before:
<Text component="p" className="pf-v6-u-mb-sm">
          <BlueInfoCircleIcon className="pf-v6-u-mr-sm" />
          {t(`You can add ${remainingTags} more tags`)}
        </Text>

content.text: Let me verify the final state of the file:

The file looks correct. No other references to `Text` remain, and the `Content` component is properly imported and used with the same props.

## Changes Applied

1. **Line 6 — Import**: Replaced `Text` with `Content` in the `@patternfly/react-core` import statement.
2. **Lines 62, 65 — JSX**: Replaced `<Text component="p" className="pf-v6-u-mb-sm">` / `</Text>` with `<Content component="p" className="pf-v6-u-mb-sm">` / `</Content>`.

All props (`component`, `className`, children) map 1:1 from `Text` to `Content`, so behavior is preserved. No other references to `Text` existed in the file, and no dead imports or declarations remain.
